flashcards.front: What is a friend class in C++?
flashcards.back: A friend class is a class that is granted access to the private and protected members of another class. It allows controlled access without breaking encapsulation entirely.

flashcards.front: What is the syntax to declare a friend class in C++?
flashcards.back: Inside the class granting friendship: friend class ClassName; This declaration can be placed in public, protected, or private section.

flashcards.front: Can a friend class access private members of the class that granted friendship?
flashcards.back: Yes, a friend class can access all private and protected members (but not private members of other classes, even if they are friends).
No, friendship is NOT inherited. If class A makes B a friend, the derived classes of B do NOT get access to A's private members.
No, friendship is NOT transitive. If A is friend of B, and B is friend of C, then A does NOT automatically become friend of C.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a friend class and a friend function?
flashcards.back: A friend class gives entire class access to private members, while a friend function gives access to only a specific function. Friend class provides broader access.

flashcards.front: Can a friend class access static members of the class that granted friendship?
flashcards.back: Yes, a friend class can access static (class) members along with all other private and protected members.

flashcards.front: What is one-way friendship in C++?
flashcards.back: Friendship is one-way: if class A declares B as friend, B can access A's private members, but A cannot access B's private members unless B also declares A as friend.

flashcards.front: Where can the friend declaration be placed in a class?
flashcards.back: The friend declaration can be placed in any access specifier section (public, protected, or private) - the location does not affect its functionality.

flashcards.front: Can we have multiple friend classes in C++?
flashcards.back: Yes, a class can declare multiple other classes as friends, and each friend class will have access to its private members.

flashcards.front: What are the advantages of using friend classes?
flashcards.back: Advantages: allows sharing private data between related classes, enables operator overloading, maintains encapsulation better than making all members public.

flashcards.front: What are the disadvantages of friend classes?
flashcards.back: Disadvantages: breaks encapsulation, creates tight coupling between classes, makes code harder to maintain, and violates OOP principles if overused.

flashcards.front: Can a friend class access constructors of the granting class?
flashcards.back: Yes, a friend class can access all constructors (including private and protected ones) of the class that granted friendship.

flashcards.front: What happens if a nested class is declared as friend?
flashcards.back: If a nested class is declared as friend, it can access private members. However, the nested class must be fully defined before being declared as friend.

flashcards.front: When should friend classes be preferred over public getters/setters?
flashcards.back: Friend classes are preferred when two classes need tight collaboration, when implementing operator overloading, or when getters/setters would be unnecessarily verbose.
